LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS MICHAEL ALFARO, DEBORAH ARBASETTI, TITAN MANAGEMENT GROUP, LLC, AND AZTECH MAINTENANCE/DEVELOPMENT, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 10, 2015 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MARCH 12, 2015 (NOT MARCH 10) AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Set For Trial On May-02-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANTS SLATS INVESTORS, LLC, THOMAS MONAHAN, ROBIN MILLER and MONAHAN PACIFIC CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT - The demurrer is overruled. In ruling on a demurrer, courts must take all material facts pleaded as true. (See Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 992, 998.) Section 7031 of the Business and Professions Code precludes an unlicensed contractor "engaged in the business or acting in the capacity of a contractor" from bringing an action in law or equity to collect for compensation for services requiring a license. "The purpose of the licensing law is to protect the public from incompetence and dishonesty in those who provide building and construction services. The licensing requirements provide minimal assurance that all persons offering such services have the requisite skill and character, understand applicable local laws and codes, and know the rudiments of administering a contracting business. (Hydrotech Systems, Ltd. v. Oasis Waterpark (1991) 52 Cal.3d 988, 995 [277 Cal.Rptr. 517, 803 P.2d 370]; Home Depot, U.S.A., Inc. v. Contractors' State License Bd. (1996) 41 Cal.App.4th 1592, 1598 [49 Cal.Rptr.2d 302].) To effect this purpose, the law need apply only to those who actually perform or supervise the performance of construction services; it need not apply to those who only supply materials to be used by others or laborers who will be supervised by others." (Contractors Labor Pool, Inc. v. Westway Contractors, Inc., (1997) 53 Cal. App. 4th 152, 164.) Here, the Cross-Complaint alleges cross-complainant agreed to: (1) supply laborers to the project; (2) perform project management services; and (3) advance costs. (Cross-Complaint 23.) Thus, as pleaded, the cross-complaint is not barred under Business and Professions Code Section 7031. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
